title: Xyathone
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Substance |image= |name=Xyathone |locations=[[Ord Cestus/Legends|Ord Cestus]]''[[The Cestus Deception]]'' |date= |discovered= |creators= |created= |type=Chemical |color= |properties=Distinct smell and taste |uses=Dilute [[Spice/Legends|narcotic]] [[Mushroom/Legends|mushrooms]] to reduce their potency and quality |cost= }} {{Quote|Did you sell my guests the mushroom?''"
"''Yes, of course. Nothing but the best—''"
"''And why then has it been cut with Xyathone?''"
"''What? I did not know, I swear—|Trillot interrogates Guntar|}} '''Xyathone''' was a chemical with a distinct taste and smell that could be used to dilute [[Spice/Legends|narcotic]] [[Mushroom/Legends|mushrooms]]. This occurred in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] in the [[X'Ting]] [[Crime lord/Legends|gangster]] [[Trillot]]'s [[Trillot's facility|facility]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Cestus]]. The usage of Xyathone greatly displeased Trillot as it reduced the potency and quality of the drug. Furious with the [[Zeetsa]] [[Guntar]], who had sold the contaminated drugs to the facility's [[Chadra-Fan/Legends|Chadra-Fan]] customers, Trillot provided recompense to the Chadra-Fan while severely punishing Guntar. ==Description== {{Quote|You should have smelled it. Tasted it in the mixing.|Trillot challenges Guntar's claimed innocence.|}} Xyathone was a chemical with a distinct smell and taste. It could be incorporated with other substances, such as certain [[Mushroom/Legends|mushrooms]] used as a [[Spice/Legends|drug]]. ==History== {{Quote|Do you say that that insignificant nose and tongue of yours aren't up to the task?''"
"''I… I suppose…''"
"''You know how I loathe inefficiency. See that the offending organs are removed.|Trillot condemns Guntar's capabilities and punishes him.|}} Around [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The Cestus Deception]]'' to c. [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. [[X'Ting]] [[Crime lord/Legends|Gang leader]] [[Trillot]] was escorting his younger [[Trillot's brood|broodmate]] [[Fizzik]] through his [[Trillot's facility|hidden facility]] on the [[Plant/Legends|planet]] [[Ord Cestus/Legends|Ord Cestus]]. As the brothers made their way through the facility, Trillot detected the scent of Xyathone in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|air]], displeasing the criminal leader. The group proceeded to an alcove where a pair [[Chadra-Fan/Legends|Chadra-Fan]] were imbibing upon some mushrooms from a [[Temperature/Legends|boiling]] [[Flask/Legends|flask]]. The mushrooms had been contaminated with Xyathone, reducing the potency and quality of the drug. Trillot summoned [[Guntar]], a [[Zeetsa]] who had sold the Chadra-Fan guests the mushrooms. Trillot briefly interrogated Guntar, challenging the Zeetsa's claims that the mushrooms were of the highest quality. When Guntar proclaimed his{{CSWECite|I|376|Guntar}} innocence, the gangster noted that the Zeetsa should have detected the contaminant in either the smell or the taste of the product. As Guntar's [[Nose/Legends|nose]] and [[Mouth/Legends|tongue]] had proven incapable of ensuring quality mushrooms, Trillot ordered his guards to remove the offending organs to Guntar's horror. To provide recompense to the Chadra-Fan, Trillot ordered additional guards to provide them mushrooms from his personal store. ==Behind the scenes== Xyathone first appeared in ''[[The Cestus Deception]]'', a [[2004]] novel written by [[Steven Barnes]] as part of the [[Clone Wars multimedia project]]. The scene featuring xyathone was cut from the abridged [[The Cestus Deception (abridged audiobook)|audiobook]], released in [[2007]] and performed by [[Jonathan Davis]]. The audiobook notes Fizzik's intent to provide information to Trillot upon entry to the facility, before skipping this scene to continue with the next chapter.[[The Cestus Deception (abridged audiobook)|''The Cestus Deception'' abridged audiobook]] ==Appearances== *''[[The Cestus Deception]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|I||Guntar}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Drugs and medicine]]
